citation: Cormier, J., & Fail Faster. (2024). Design Your Destiny.
summary:
# Summary

The provided source text functions as a promotional and informational document regarding "Design Your Destiny: Running Out of Time," an interactive educational product created by Jay Cormier and Fail Faster. The text is directed primarily at teachers and librarians, positioning the product as a resource for integrating game design principles into library services and classroom settings. The core premise of the product is described as an interactive time-travel story that encapsulates multiple games and an activity book. Within this narrative framework, participants encounter games that are intentionally "broken" or flawed. The text asserts that the accompanying book provides insights and guidance on how to repair these mechanical issues, requiring users to implement their own solutions and playtest their revised versions to assist the main characters in the story. This structure suggests a pedagogical approach focused on iterative design, problem-solving, and critical analysis of game mechanics.

The document does not present empirical data, research findings, or statistical analysis. Instead, it serves as a hub for resources related to the product and its creator. It references a Video on Demand (VOD) session hosted on Twitch, indicating that the content was likely presented in a webinar format aimed at library professionals. The text explicitly lists resources for libraries to promote the title, including posters and image files. Furthermore, it connects "Design Your Destiny" to Jay Cormier’s broader educational initiatives, specifically the "Fail Faster" online game design course, suggesting that this product is part of a larger ecosystem of game development education.

A significant portion of the text is dedicated to curating related materials and tools, which may serve as supplementary resources for librarians implementing games-based services. These include references to Cormier’s other works (e.g., "MIND MGMT," "Harrow County"), articles on setting up tabletop role-playing games (TTRPGs) in libraries, and guides on game jams. The text also lists specific game design software such as Twine, RPG Maker, Unity, and Godot, alongside tutorials and reference guides. This curation implies a recommendation for librarians to familiarize themselves with these tools to support patrons interested in game creation.

Additionally, the source text emphasizes community standards and safety within gaming environments. It includes links to TTRPG safety toolkits, such as "Script Change" and "Lines and Veils," which are protocols used to ensure participant comfort and consent during role-playing activities. The inclusion of a Twitch chat policy for "GameRT" (likely the Game Round Table or similar library gaming initiative) underscores the importance of respectful interaction and moderation in digital and physical gaming spaces. The text also provides comparative media recommendations, listing "Play Alikes," "Watch Alikes," and "Read Alikes" to help contextualize the narrative style and themes of "Design Your Destiny" for potential users.

In the context of Games-Based Library Services (GBLS), this document highlights the intersection of commercial educational products and library programming. It suggests that libraries can leverage structured, narrative-driven game design activities to engage patrons in STEM-related skills, specifically focusing on logic, iteration, and creative problem-solving. The explicit address to librarians and the provision of promotional materials indicate a targeted effort to integrate this resource into library collections or programs. However, as the text is purely descriptive and promotional, it lacks evidence of efficacy, user engagement metrics, or pedagogical outcomes. It serves as a catalog entry and resource guide rather than an evaluation of the product's impact on learning or community building.
sourceText:
“Design Your Destiny” by Jay Cormier and Fail Faster
Design Your Destiny: Running Out of Time is an interactive time travel story, multiple games and an activity book all wrapped up in one package. Throughout the story you'll be asked to play a game using included components, but each game will be broken in some way. The book then provides insights and guidance on ways you could fix these problems. You'll be asked to implement your own ideas and playtest your version of these games as you try to help our main characters through their story.
Attention Teachers & Librarians!!

Watch the VOD of this session here: https://www.twitch.tv/videos/1926379610
The Game Design Your Destiny by Fail Faster
● Resources for libraries to help spread the word about Design Your Destiny ○ Poster and jpg
The Creator Jay Cormier on Board Game Geek
● Fail Faster, Jay Cormier’s Online Game Design Course ○ Online Fail Faster Game Design Program
● Jay Cormier’s Other Work ○ Off the Page Games ● MIND MGMT on BGG ● Harrow County on BGG ● Manifest Destiny on BGG
Actual Play ● DESIGN YOUR DESTINY: RUNNING OUT OF TIME | Kickstarter Preview | Fail Faster
Articles, Books, and More Info ● Let's Roll: A Guide to Setting up Tabletop Role-Playing Games in your School or Public Library ● Indie Games: From Dream to Delivery ● The Game Jam Survival Guide ● Game Jams – History, Technology, and Organisation ● Game Jam Tips ● International Conference on Game Jams, Hackathons, and Game Creation Events ● The Game Jam Guide (Free Curriculum ePub download from Carnegie Mellon UP) ● Game Maker’s Toolkit (YouTube) ● The Best Games for Learning Game Development [2020] | Ask Gamedev
Game Design Tools ● Twine ○ Twine Reference Guide ● RPG Maker

○ RPG Maker Tutorials ○ RPG Maker MZ The Basics (Sponsored by Komodo) ● Unity ● Godot ● DIY Adventure Games ● The One-Page RPG Generator | PS Berge (grave snail games)
Play Alikes ● The Ultimate Micro-RPG Book: 40 Fast, Easy, and Fun Tabletop Games ● Cloud Dungeon ● Honey Heist | Grant Howitt ● Lasers and Feelings | John Harper ● Game Template | Oliver Darkshire (a free template Oliver Darkshire created and uses for many of his one-page RPGs)
Watch Alikes Doctor Who Red Dwarf The Umbrella Academy (Netflix) Russian Doll (Netflix) Groundhog Day Palm Springs (Hulu) Before I Fall Happy Death Day
Read Alikes The Eyre Affair, by Jasper Fforde Slaughterhouse-Five, by Kurt Vonnegut Before the Coffee Gets Cold, by Toshikazu Kawaguchi This is How You Lose the Time War, by Amal El-Mohtar and Max Gladstone A Wrinkle in Time, by Madeleine L’Engle Miss Peregrine’s Home for Peculiar Children, by Ransom Riggs Oona Out of Order, by Margarita Montimore 11/22/63, by Stephen King The 7 ½ Deaths of Evelyn Hardcastle, by Stuart Turton The Invisible Life of Addie LaRue, by V. E. Schwab

Safety Tools ● Script Change by Beau Jágr Sheldon ● Guest Blog: An Introduction to Lines and Veils ● The Door is Open by Eirik Fatland ○ Blog by Lizzie Stark ○ Blog 2 by Eirik Fatland ● TTRPG Safety Toolkit collected by Kienna Shaw and Lauren Bryant-Monk ○ TTRPG Safety Toolkit Guide ○ Google Drive
Game RT Twitch Policy 1. Be kind and respectful to one another 2. Hate speech of any type will not be tolerated 3. We reserve the right to remove anyone from the chat for any reason 4. If you are feeling harassed by anyone, please notify a GameRT twitch mod and one of us will assist you 5. If you'd rather talk privately about anything, please send us a direct message
